title: Macramé necklace set
subtitle: Three simple knotted cord necklaces with bead accents, using spiral half-hitch and square knot patterns
excerpt: Make a set of three macramé necklaces in fine cotton or waxed linen cord: one in a square knot chevron, one in a spiral half-hitch, and one with a simple knotted bead pendant. Each necklace takes under an hour and is a good first project for beginners with the individual knot techniques.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional macramé knotting practice. Knotted cord jewellery is documented in mid-twentieth-century craft manuals and in contemporary British macramé teaching.

glossaryTerms.definition: A simple loop of one cord over another. Repeated half hitches in one direction create the spiral (half-hitch spiral) necklace. Alternating left and right half hitches create a square knot.
A flat, symmetrical knot made with two working cords around two filler cords. The foundation knot for the chevron necklace in this set.
A folded-cord mounting knot used to attach cords to a ring or another cord. The starting point for all three necklaces in this set.

content.text: Necklaces are one of the fastest macramé projects because the cords are short and the knot count is manageable in a single sitting. The set covers three structures: the
bead mount. Use 1 to 1.5 mm waxed linen or fine cotton for the necklaces; thicker cord makes the knots too large for jewellery scale.

items.name: Waxed linen or fine cotton cord, 1 to 1.5 mm
Round wooden or ceramic beads, 8 to 12 mm hole
Small clasp or extra cord for tie closure

content.text: Cut 2 cords each 1.5 metres long. Fold one cord in half for the 2 filler cords; fold the other cord in half for the 2 working cords. Work only left half-hitches: pass the left working cord over the filler cords from left to right, then back under and up through the loop from right to left. Continue in one direction for 20 cm. The repeated single-direction half hitches produce a spiral twist automatically. Tie off the ends and add a bead at each end of the central filler before knotting the final closure.

Cut 4 cords each 1.2 metres long. Group in two pairs of 2 filler and 2 working cords. Work alternating square knots for 25 cm. On each square knot, keep the tension even so the flat square knot pattern reads clearly across the cord width. Finish both ends with a sliding or fixed closure.

Cut 2 cords, each 1.5 metres long. Thread the centre point of both cords through a bead. Below the bead, work 3 square knots on all 4 cords to hold the bead in place. On each side of the bead, work simple overhand knots every 3 cm along the cord for a knotted chain effect extending to the necklace length.

items.problem: The half-hitch spiral is not twisting
items.cause: Both left and right half hitches are being mixed, which cancels out the spiral and produces a flat chain instead
items.fix: Use only left half hitches (or only right half hitches) throughout the spiral section. The twist builds up over repeated half hitches in one direction; alternating directions produces a square knot effect instead.

items.problem: The necklace cords are tangling constantly while working
items.cause: The long cords are not managed as the knotting progresses
items.fix: Roll up the excess cord on each working cord and secure with a loose overhand knot or a cord bobbin. Unroll a few centimetres at a time as needed.
